message:
test(conformance): add parity_gate.yaml for FP-P2D-064..FP-P2D-125

Registers 62 new phase2c parity packet gate definitions covering
the conformance matrix expansion that follows the IO round-trip
and reshape / pivot work. Each parity_gate.yaml pins:

- packet_id = FP-P2D-0xx so the runner can look up the corresponding
  fixture set.
- strict critical_drift_budget = 0, strict
  non_critical_drift_budget_percent = 0.1 - the standard strict
  contract inherited from FP-P2D-060..063 so no new packet can
  introduce silent strict drift.
- hardened divergence_budget_percent = 1.0 with no allowlisted
  categories (packets that need category allow-lists can opt in
  once the implementation lands).
- machine_check.suite = phase2c_packets + require_fixture_count_
  at_least: 3 + require_failed: 0, matching how FP-P2D-060..063
  gate themselves.

Only the gate YAML is committed. The parity_gate_result.json,
parity_mismatch_corpus.json, parity_report.*.json, and
parity_report.raptorq.json files in each directory are runtime
outputs and are left untracked to match the precedent set by the
earlier FP-P2D-0xx packets. The gates themselves currently
report failed=3 because the backing implementations are still
being wired up - opening the packets in-repo is the explicit
signal that those implementations are expected next.

files.patch:
@@ -0,0 +1,11 @@
+packet_id: FP-P2D-064
+strict:
+  critical_drift_budget: 0
+  non_critical_drift_budget_percent: 0.1
+hardened:
+  divergence_budget_percent: 1.0
+  allowlisted_divergence_categories: null
+machine_check:
+  suite: phase2c_packets
+  require_fixture_count_at_least: 3
+  require_failed: 0
